name: Forest and Pine Grove Trail PRC-BU 72
name_original: Castillo de Peñaranda de Duero — circular desde San Juan del Monte
description: Jog a moderate 7.2-mile (11.6 km) circular route from San Juan del Monte, exploring Peñaranda de Duero Castle and scenic riverside paths.
region_intro: Jogging routes around San Juan Del Monte offer a mix of natural landscapes and urban green spaces. The region features varied terrain, including areas with gentle inclines and more challenging hills. These running paths provide opportunities to experience both forested sections and open areas suitable for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around San Juan Del Monte The most popular jogging route is Forest and Pine Grove Trail PRC-BU 72, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a gradual ascent through forested areas. Another top favourite among local runners is Way of the Cid: Castillejo de Robledo Loop, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This loop offers a mix of open terrain and gentle hills, ideal for a steady run. Local runners also love the Running loop from Fresnillo de las Dueñas, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 44 minutes. Jogging around San Juan Del Monte is defined by forested sections, open fields, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more difficult challenges. The routes in San Juan Del Monte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 25 runners have used komoot to explore San Juan Del Monte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find around San Juan Del Monte?
A: The area around San Juan Del Monte offers a diverse range of running routes, from moderate paths through forested sections and pine groves to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes. You'll find options that traverse open fields and gentle hills, catering to various fitness levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050663545/forest-and-pine-grove-trail-prc-bu-72" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest and Pine Grove Trail PRC-BU 72&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate experience through woodlands.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging running trails in the region?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding run, the region features longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Duero – Peñaranda de Duero Castle loop from Pico de la Vid&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 36.2 km (22.5 miles) trail with substantial elevation gain, offering a significant challenge for experienced runners.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around San Juan Del Monte?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running paths in the area are designed as loops, perfect for starting and ending your run in the same spot. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064160854/way-of-the-cid-castillejo-de-robledo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way of the Cid: Castillejo de Robledo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Fresnillo de las Dueñas&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on these running trails?
A: Runners in San Juan Del Monte can enjoy a mix of natural and historical scenery. Trails often lead through forested sections, open fields, and areas with gentle hills. You might also encounter historical settlements and structures. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064160854/way-of-the-cid-castillejo-de-robledo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way of the Cid: Castillejo de Robledo Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo de Robledo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Many routes offer glimpses of historical landmarks. You can find trails that pass by charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Langa de Duero&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, as well as significant structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3395685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Vid&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How popular are the running routes in San Juan Del Monte with other users?
A: The running routes in San Juan Del Monte are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Over 25 runners have already explored the varied terrain using komoot, indicating a positive experience among those who have visited.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for jogging in this area?
A: The terrain for jogging around San Juan Del Monte is quite varied. You'll encounter forested sections, open fields, and gentle to moderate hills. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050663545/forest-and-pine-grove-trail-prc-bu-72" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest and Pine Grove Trail PRC-BU 72&lt;/a&gt;, feature gradual ascents, while others offer a mix of flat and undulating paths, providing a good workout for different preferences.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: While specific details on waterside views are not extensively highlighted for all routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calabaza – Los Gatos loop from Río Duero&lt;/a&gt; indicates proximity to the Duero River, suggesting potential opportunities for running alongside or with views of this significant waterway.

Q: What is the longest running route available in San Juan Del Monte?
A: The longest running route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Duero – Peñaranda de Duero Castle loop from Pico de la Vid&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 36.2 kilometers (22.5 miles). This difficult trail is ideal for long-distance training or an extended adventure.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a quick run, under an hour?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Fresnillo de las Dueñas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.0 km (4.3 miles) trail that can typically be completed in about 44 minutes, making it perfect for a brisk workout.

Q: How many running routes are there in total for San Juan Del Monte?
A: There are at least 6 distinct running routes documented around San Juan Del Monte, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for joggers and runners.
tips:
Peñaranda de Duero is a municipality in Burgos, in the Ribera del Duero region, known for its rich historical heritage. Its old town, declared a Historic Site, is notable for the Peñaranda de Duero Castle, the Palace of the Counts of Miranda, its arcaded Plaza Mayor, and its provincial roll.
In addition to its architecture, the town is part of the Ribera del Duero Designation of Origin and has a strong winemaking tradition.

Peñaranda de Duero Castle is a well-preserved Gothic castle located in Peñaranda de Duero, in the province of Burgos. Originally built in the 10th century, it was significantly renovated in the 15th century by the Counts of Miranda del Castañar.
The castle features a four-storey keep with a roof terrace, battlements and a gallery of machicolations. It stands at the start of a defensive wall that once surrounded the town, of which only the crenellated arch of "Las Monjas" remains.
Small hermitage with a stone facade that is very close to the town of San Juan del Monte.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Castillo de Peñaranda de Duero — circular from San Juan del Monte route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 20 minutes to complete, covering a distance of about 11.6 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate. It's suitable for runners with a reasonable level of fitness, offering a mix of terrain and some inclines.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied landscapes, including agricultural lands, forests of poplars and junipers, and riverside paths along the Arandilla River. The path leading up to the castle may involve inclines and possibly uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the impressive Peñaranda de Duero Castle, offering panoramic views. You'll also pass through the picturesque medieval village of Peñaranda de Duero and near the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Vega. The route showcases the blend of historical architecture and natural beauty of the Ribera del Duero region.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions are not noted, trails in natural areas like this are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near agricultural areas, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for jogging?
faqs.answer: The Ribera del Duero region experiences warm summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, with milder weather and beautiful scenery. Early mornings in summer can also be suitable to avoid the heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to jog or hike this circular route. Access to the natural areas and the village is generally free. There might be an entrance fee if you wish to enter the castle's Interpretation Centre.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour starts from San Juan del Monte, Peñaranda de Duero itself, being a village, likely offers public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the village center or close to the castle if you plan to start your loop from there.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths, including parts of the 'Sendero del bosque y del pinar', 'Sello Camino del Cid', and 'Camino de la Ermita'.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the village of Peñaranda de Duero, you will find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities there. It's a good place to refuel before or after your run.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length of nearly 12 kilometers, it might be challenging for very young children to jog the entire route. However, families with older children who are accustomed to moderate physical activity could enjoy it, especially for the historical and scenic aspects.
